title: The Trust and Stability Ceremony
subtitle: A weekly ritual for anchoring unshakeable trust in your relationship with money
excerpt: A weekly ritual ceremony from Week 10 of the Money Journal program. Works through the five-part ceremony shape with the specific intention of releasing every remaining trace of fear or control around money and allowing deep, settled trust.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Week 10 Ritual in MONEY Journal (Rebecca Page, 2025). Used with permission as part of the homemade.education Mindset library.

practice.whenToUse: Use once at the start of a week when the habit of managing, controlling, or bracing around money is running at a higher level than usual.

content.text: A weekly ritual for the grip — the constant checking, the bracing for the next problem, the inability to let money sit in the account without a plan attached to it. Works through the five-part ceremony shape: prepare, release, allow, integrate, anchor.
Sit somewhere peaceful. Picture yourself standing on steady ground — not braced, not alert, not waiting for the next thing to go wrong. Just standing.
I am ready to release every last trace of fear, instability, or control around money. I release it now. I release it now. I release it now.
I am ready to align to and allow deep, unshakable trust in money, in life, and in myself. I allow it now. I allow it now. I allow it now.
Take a slow breath in and all the way out. Picture the ground beneath you as completely solid — your finances, your home, your capacity to handle whatever comes. Steady.
Write this anchor statement in your own handwriting. Say it aloud:
This week, trust and ease define my relationship with money.
Adapted from the Week 10 Ritual in MONEY Journal (Rebecca Page, 2025). Part of the 12-week Money Journal ceremony series.
